title: Accessibility
drDigitalIntro:
Accessibility settings make your computer easier for everyone to use.
• Zoom enlarges the screen; Pointer size makes the cursor bigger.
• A screen reader reads text aloud.
• Captions add subtitles. Use whatever helps you work comfortably.

playgroundTask.instructions: Someone needs a bigger cursor. Open Accessibility settings and turn on Pointer Size (Larger Text).

drDigitalSuccess: Accessibility settings are in System Settings > Accessibility — always there when you need them.
drDigitalHint: Open Accessibility from the sidebar and look for the Larger Text toggle.
